notes_md:
**ăn kẹo** means “to eat candy.” It’s a simple verb phrase: *ăn* (to eat) + *kẹo* (candy).

You use it like other food verbs: **ăn + food** (ăn cơm, ăn bánh, ăn kẹo). It often goes with time phrases (sau bữa ăn, buổi tối) or frequency words (hay, thường, ít khi).


Related word: **đồ ngọt** (sweets, sugary food) — so *ăn đồ ngọt* is broader than just candy.
notes:
ăn kẹo means “to eat candy.” It’s a simple verb phrase: ăn (to eat) + kẹo (candy).

You use it like other food verbs: ăn + food (ăn cơm, ăn bánh, ăn kẹo). It often goes with time phrases (sau bữa ăn, buổi tối) or frequency words (hay, thường, ít khi).

Related word: đồ ngọt (sweets, sugary food) — so ăn đồ ngọt is broader than just candy.
exampleSentencesTranslated: I like to eat candy after dinner when watching movies with my family.
I remember when I was young, I often went out to the park with friends to eat candy.
She usually does not let children eat candy before meals so they don’t get full.
exampleSentences: Em thích ăn kẹo sau bữa tối khi xem phim với gia đình.
Anh nhớ hồi nhỏ hay cùng bạn bè ra ngoài công viên để ăn kẹo.
Chị thường không cho trẻ con ăn kẹo trước bữa ăn để các bé không bị no.
